tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The fail-to-pass tests extract the ondata function, eval it, and actually execute it with mock objects that throw errors, checking that dest.destroy is called and errors don't escape. The pass-to-pass tests similarly execute the function. test_ondata_not_stub checks both text content and meaningful structure. This is primarily behavioral testing.

test_not_tautological.reason: A stub ondata that does nothing would fail test_normal_write_still_works (written===null check) and test_backpressure_pause (paused not called). A stub that just calls dest.write without try/catch would fail test_write_error_caught_and_forwarded (threwUncaught would be true). The tests require actual error-catching logic.

The main behaviors are covered.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with clear section headers (Gate, Fail-to-pass, Pass-to-pass, Anti-stub), descriptive function names and docstrings explaining what each test verifies.
